seoTitle: How to Overlay Images and Text in Canva
metaDescription: Layer text and images together in Canva to create overlays that add depth and highlight key details in social posts and marketing graphics.
intro: Overlaying text and images adds depth to a design and helps highlight important information, whether you're building a social post, a promotional graphic, or a presentation slide. This guide covers the basics of creating overlays in Canva and applying them to real design projects.

steps.heading: Understand how overlays work in Canva
steps.body: An overlay is created by layering one element, like text or a shape, on top of another, such as a background image, to add contrast or emphasis.

steps.heading: Add your base image or background
steps.body: Start with the image or color background you want to build the overlay on top of.

steps.heading: Layer text or graphics on top
steps.body: Add text boxes, shapes, or icons over the background and adjust their transparency or color so they stand out clearly.

steps.heading: Fine-tune the overlay for visual balance
steps.body: Adjust opacity, positioning, and contrast so the overlaid elements are readable without overwhelming the base image.

steps.heading: Apply overlays to your real projects
steps.body: Use this layering technique across social posts, marketing materials, and presentations to add visual interest and highlight key messages.

faq.question: Can I adjust the transparency of an overlay in Canva?
faq.answer: Yes, Canva lets you control the opacity of layered elements so overlays can be subtle or bold depending on the design.

faq.question: Is creating overlays in Canva beginner-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, the layering process uses simple drag-and-drop steps that work well for beginners and experienced designers alike.

faq.question: What's a common use case for overlays in marketing?
faq.answer: Overlays are often used to add text captions or branding on top of photos for social media posts and promotional graphics.

faq.question: Can I overlay multiple elements at once?
faq.answer: Yes, you can stack several layers of text, shapes, and images to build a more complex overlay design.
